title: Fix linting
body:
A series of commits to:
* Update golangci-lint and make it initially pass. There was a series of issues preventing from running it cleanly (e.g., broken contrib/perftest and upstream regressions). Pinning it to 1.18.0 for now.
* Use a blacklisting approach for linters instead of enabling them one-by-one. This makes it easier to maintain (updates will pull in new linters automatically) and it's easier to enable disabled linters.
* Last but not least, start by enabling `gocritic`.

@baude @mheon @rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat PTAL

comments.body:
> level=error msg="Running error: no such linter \"funlen\""

Looks like the CI has another golangci-lint already installed

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe,vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> > level=error msg="Running error: no such linter "funlen""
> 
> Looks like the CI has another golangci-lint already installed

@cevich, `quay.io/libpod/gate:master` ships with the tools from `make install.tools`. Are you cool with removing that from the Dockerfile? This way, the gating tests will always fetch the versions the Makefile wants. I didn't want to update and overwrite the image before consulting you.
LGTM once the CI and cevich are happy
I'll defer merge until we get a response from Chris.

> I'll defer merge until we get a response from Chris.

I think we can merge as I did not change the Dockerfile (or the image). To make it pass for now, I added an `rm $GOPATH/bin/*` to the gating tasks.
Manually adding label so we merge

Sorry I'm late to the party as usual :disappointed: 

> quay.io/libpod/gate:master ships with the tools from make install.tools...cool with removing that from the Dockerfile?

Yes, please make whatever changes are needed.  My understanding is the only user of that image is 'gate_task'.  The purpose of both is to reliably pass or fail as quickly as possible.  Though quickly and consistently failing, is typically undesirable :smile:  So installing at runtime is fine if the linter is a moving target.
